content:
배수원 교수는 부산대학교 기계공학부에 재직중입니다.
부산대학교에서 근무하기 전, 브룩헤이븐 국립연구소의 기능성 나노소재센터에서 연구를 수행하였습니다. 대한민국 서울에 위치한 한양대학교와 뉴욕 이타카에 위치한 코넬대학교에서 기계공학 학사와 박사 학위를 받았습니다.
summary:
배수원 교수는 부산대학교 기계공학부에 재직중입니다.
부산대학교에서 근무하기 전, 브룩헤이븐 국립연구소의 기능성 나노소재센터에서 연구를 수행하였습니다. 대한민국 서울에 위치한 한양대학교와 뉴욕 이타카에 위치한 코넬대학교에서 기계공학 학사와 박사 학위를 받았습니다.
title: 배수원

content:
우리 연구실 배수원 교수가 연구책임자로 한국연구재단 개인기초 신진연구(유형B)에 선정되었습니다.
2026년 3월부터 2031년 2월까지 “유도 자기조립(DSA) 공정 최적화를 위한 기판 명시형(Explicit Substrate) 입자 기반 분자동역학 시뮬레이션 프레임워크 및 자율 실험 플랫폼 개발”이라는 과제명으로 반도체 초미세 패터닝 공정 최적화를 위한 입자 기반 분자동역학 프레임워크 개발하고 DSA 결함 제어 메커니즘 규명하는 연구를 수행합니다.
과제정보)
내역사업명: 우수연구-신진연구(유형B)
과제번호: RS-2026-25493211
과제명: 유도 자기조립(DSA) 공정 최적화를 위한 기판 명시형(Explicit Substrate) 입자 기반 분자동역학 시뮬레이션 프레임워크 및 자율 실험 플랫폼 개발
총연구기간: 2026-03-01 - 2031-02-28
summary:
우리 연구실 배수원 교수가 연구책임자로 한국연구재단 개인기초 신진연구(유형B)에 선정되었습니다.
2026년 3월부터 2031년 2월까지 “유도 자기조립(DSA) 공정 최적화를 위한 기판 명시형(Explicit Substrate) 입자 기반 분자동역학 시뮬레이션 프레임워크 및 자율 실험 플랫폼 개발”이라는 과제명으로 반도체 초미세 패터닝 공정 최적화를 위한 입자 기반 분자동역학 프레임워크 개발하고 DSA 결함 제어 메커니즘 규명하는 연구를 수행합니다.
title: 2026년도 개인기초 신진연구(유형B) 선정

content:
우리 연구실이 참여한 “2차원 소재 동적 마찰 계면 트라이볼로지 기초연구실”이 한국연구재단 2025년도 글로벌 기초연구실에 선정되었습니다.
연구책임자인 기계공학부 김송길 교수님과 더불어 반치범 교수님, 서준호 교수님 연구실과 함께 2025년 6월 1일부터 2028년 5월 31일까지 공동 연구를 수행합니다.
과제정보)
내역사업명: 글로벌 기초연구실(심화)
과제번호: RS-2025-02216195
과제명: 2차원 소재 동적 마찰 계면 트라이볼로지 기초연구실
총연구기간: 2025-06-01 - 2028-05-31
summary:
우리 연구실이 참여한 “2차원 소재 동적 마찰 계면 트라이볼로지 기초연구실”이 한국연구재단 2025년도 글로벌 기초연구실에 선정되었습니다.
연구책임자인 기계공학부 김송길 교수님과 더불어 반치범 교수님, 서준호 교수님 연구실과 함께 2025년 6월 1일부터 2028년 5월 31일까지 공동 연구를 수행합니다.
title: 2025년도 글로벌 기초연구실(BRL) 선정

content: 부산대학교 기계공학부의 BAE 연구실 홈페이지가 개설되었습니다.
summary: 부산대학교 기계공학부의 BAE 연구실 홈페이지가 개설되었습니다.
title: BAE 연구실 홈페이지 개설

summary: Orientation of block copolymer (BCP) morphology in thin films is critical to applications as nanostructured coatings. Despite being well-studied, the ability to control BCP orientation across all possible block constituents remains challenging. Here, we deploy coarse-grained molecular dynamics simulations to study diblock copolymer ordering in thin films, focusing on chain makeup, substrate surface energy, and surface tension disparity between the two constituent blocks. We explore the multi-dimensional parameter space of ordering using a machine-learning approach, where an autonomous loop using a Gaussian process (GP) control algorithm iteratively selects high-value simulations to compute. The trained GP model serves as both a complete map of system response, and a robust means of extracting material knowledge.
title: Surface enrichment dictates block copolymer orientation

summary: The directed self-assembly (DSA) of block copolymers (BCPs) is a powerful approach to fabricate complex nanostructure arrays, but finding morphologies that emerge with changes in polymer architecture, composition, or assembly constraints remains daunting because of the increased dimensionality of the DSA design space. Here, we demonstrate machine-guided discovery of emergent morphologies from a cylinder/lamellae BCP blend directed by a chemical grating template, conducted without direct human intervention on a synchrotron x-ray scattering beamline.
title: Autonomous discovery of emergent morphologies in directed self-assembly of block copolymer blends

summary: Block copolymers spontaneously self-assemble into well-defined nanoscale morphologies. Yet equilibrium assembly gives rise to a limited set of structures. Non-equilibrium strategies can, in principle, expand diversity by exploiting self-assembly's responsive nature. In this vein, we developed a pathway priming strategy combining control of thin film initial configurations and ordering history. We demonstrate a library of exotic non-native morphologies, including vertically-oriented perforated lamellae, aqueduct structures, parapets, and networks of crisscrossing lamellae.
title: Priming self-assembly pathways by stacking block copolymers

summary: The nanoscale morphologies of block copolymer (BCP) thin films are determined by chain architecture. Experimental studies of thin film blends of different BCP chain types have demonstrated that blending can stabilize new motifs, such as coexistence phases. Here, we deploy coarse-grained molecular dynamics (MD) simulations in order to better understand the self-assembly behavior of BCP blend thin films. Our simulations show that there are local concentration deviations at the scale of the morphological objects that dictate the local structure, and that BCP chains redistribute within the morphology so as to stabilize the structure.
title: Chain Redistribution Stabilizes Coexistence Phases in Block Copolymer Blends

summary: We report thickness and homopolymer molar mass dependencies in thin film blends of a compositionally symmetric lamellar polystyrene-block-poly(methyl methacrylate) diblock copolymer with near-equal volume fractions of PS and PMMA homopolymers. To efficiently explore this parameter space, we prepared combinatorial samples with gradient homopolymer concentrations using electrospray deposition and gradient film thicknesses using flow coating. Coarse-grained molecular dynamics simulations indicate that thickness-dependent orientation changes are induced by homopolymer segregation to film surfaces.
title: Film Thickness and Composition Effects in Symmetric Ternary Block Copolymer/Homopolymer Blend Films: Domain Spacing and Orientation

summary: An enhancement in the mechanical properties of poly(butylmethacrylate) is induced by folding the polymer chains through covalent intramolecular crosslinking. Intramolecular crosslinking causes an increase in intramolecular interactions and inhibition of intermolecular interactions. An intermediate intramolecular crosslinking degree shows optimal mechanical properties. Molecular dynamics simulations are used to confirm and provide molecular mechanisms to explain the experimental results.
title: Highly Stretchable Polymers: Mechanical Properties Improvement by Balancing Intra‐ and Intermolecular Interactions

summary: Molecular dynamics simulations are used to study the effects of intramolecular chemical cross-linking on chain topology and thermomechanical properties of a bulk thermoplastic polymer assembled purely from single chain polymer nanoparticles (SCPNs). Chain unfolding and entanglement were found to decrease with increasing intrachain cross-linking ratio. The simulated mechanical testing shows that the bulk polymer becomes stiffer, stronger, and more brittle as the intrachain cross-linking ratio is increased.
title: The Effect of Intrachain Cross-Linking on the Thermomechanical Behavior of Bulk Polymers Assembled Solely from Single Chain Polymer Nanoparticles

summary: Adjustable materials based on controlling the metal ligand interactions in metal containing polymers are prepared and characterized. Copper(II) carboxylate is used to crosslink a stretchable acrylic polymer, resulting in increased stiffness and strength. Moreover, modulation of the material properties is accomplished by attaching different ligands to the copper cations.
title: Modulating metallopolymer mechanical properties by controlling metal ligand crosslinking

summary: A study on the effect of intramolecular cross-linking on the mechanical properties of a glassy polymer. Mechanical testing indicates that intramolecular cross-linking does not affect the mechanical properties at the elastic region but does influence these properties at the plastic region. Interestingly, intramolecular cross-linking leads to different effects than regular interchain cross-linking, consistent with a reduction in chain entanglement.
title: Mechanical and Thermomechanical Characterization of Glassy Thermoplastics with Intrachain Cross-Links

summary: Molecular dynamics (MD) simulations are used to study thermomechanical behavior of individual single chain polymer nanoparticles (SCPNs) with different underlying structures by varying the ratio of cross-linking and the degree of polymerization. The results indicate that the constituent monomers of SCPNs become less mobile as the degree of cross-linking is increased, corresponding to lower diffusivity and higher stress at a given temperature.
title: Tailoring single chain polymer nanoparticle thermo-mechanical behavior by cross-link density
